business.headline: Are bank lockers still the safest place for your valuables?Bank lockers are trusted to store gold and valuables, but recent cases have raised questions about safety. At the same time, getting a bank locker itself has become increasingly difficult, with waiting lists stretching months or even years in many cities.

Is buying a Rs 1 lakh iPhone on EMI a bad idea? Here's the math you should knowEasy EMI options offered by banks and online platforms have made it possible to buy these expensive devices with monthly payments that may appear manageable at first glance.

Domestic LPG price hiked by Rs 60, commercial cylinder up Rs 115 amid Iran warIndustry officials said the increase reflects a surge in global energy costs triggered by tensions and disruptions in West Asia, a region critical to global oil and gas supply chains.

Wall Street slides as weak jobs data, Middle East tensions rattle marketsthe Middle East conflict pushed up oil prices the most this week since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, bringing shipping through the strategic Strait of Hormuz to a halt.

Tariff refund system to be ready in 45 days: US customs agencyThe US Supreme Court had struck down Trump's tariffs, but did ⁠not say how the collected levies should be refunded, worrying small importers that the process would be prohibitively expensive and time-consuming.

Prachar Communications' Manyavar Mohey viral campaign crosses 300 million viewsThe campaign has already generated over 8 million organic likes and has crossed 100 million cumulative views on Instagram on the celebrity collaboration post within 72 hours, marking one of the largest celebrity-led moment marketing activations in India.

Brent Crude oil hits highest since April 2024 as Middle East conflict escalatesOil markets have seen a sharp rally this week. Brent Crude has climbed about 20%, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) has surged roughly 25%.

Buying property in wife's or daughter's name? Save on stamp duty, loans and taxBuying a home is one of the biggest financial decisions many Indians make. But did you know that when a woman buys property in her own name, the purchase can come with added benefits such as lower stamp duty, cheaper home loans and useful tax deductions?

India holds sufficient crude stocks, no immediate fuel supply threat: Govt sourcesOfficials said India has sufficient stocks of petrol, diesel, LPG and natural gas. The government is closely monitoring the energy situation and reviewing it several times a day.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as Iran war lifts crude oil price; worst week in a yearThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 1,097 points to close at 78,918.90, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 315.45 points to end the day at 24,450.45.

More women homebuyers now eye Rs 90 lakh to Rs 2.5 crore homes, says ANAROCKWomen are now choosing bigger homes, targeting premium price segments, and treating property as a long-term wealth-building asset, says data.

If crude oil hits $100 per barrel, here's what it would mean for IndiaIf crude oil surges past $100 per barrel, India could face a higher import bill, a weaker rupee and rising inflation, showing how distant geopolitical conflicts can quickly ripple into fuel prices and economic stability.

Mukesh Ambani leads India's richest, Gautam Adani second: Hurun Global Rich List 2026Mukesh Ambani continues to hold the title of India's richest person and also remains Asia's wealthiest individual. His total wealth rose by about 9% over the past year to reach Rs 9.8 lakh crore.

Investing in times of war: Panic, pause or buy the dip?Sensex and Nifty have seen declines as investors reacted to rising uncertainty, volatile crude oil prices and fears of a wider conflict. For many retail investors, this has meant seeing their mutual fund or stock market portfolios slip into the red.

Building businesses that last: 5 lessons for women entrepreneursAcross the country, millions of women are running businesses in their own way. Some operate small enterprises, others are building growing ventures, but each one contributes to livelihoods, job creation, and the strength of local economies.

Sensex down 1,100 points: Why is stock market falling today?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 566.31 points at 79,449.59, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 164.85 points to 24,601.05 as of 11:24 am. Sensex and Nifty shed over half a percent.

India asks refiners to maximise LPG output, sell only to IOC, BPCL and HPCLRefiners have been directed to increase LPG output and sell the fuel only to three state-run oil marketing companies, Indian Oil Corporation, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited.

Gulf crisis disrupts LNG supply and your kitchen may feel the heatIf LNG supply disruptions persist, the impact may soon reach your kitchen, as higher global gas prices gradually filter into household gas bills and CNG costs across Indian cities.

Sensex, Nifty in red over Iran war: When will stock market bounce back?The ongoing West Asia conflict involving Iran, Israel and the US has raised fears that energy supplies could be disrupted. If oil supply tightens, crude prices may rise further.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Iran war weighs in on investor sentiment; RIL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was down 368.75 points to 79,647.15, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 115.25 points to 24,650.65 as of 9:34 am.

entertainment.headline: Vijay's wife files fresh plea amid buzz over him attending wedding with Trisha Thalapathy Vijay's wife Sangeetha Sornalingam filed a fresh petition in their ongoing divorce case. She is seeking an interim court order over her right to stay in the matrimonial home.

Fans react to Dhurandhar The Revenge trailer: You are not ready for this The trailer of Dhurandhar: The Revenge has sparked a frenzy online. Fans are sharing intense reactions to Ranveer Singh's return and the film's high-octane preview ahead of its release.

Dhurandhar The Revenge trailer: Ranveer's Hamza vows India will write Pak's future The trailer of Dhurandhar: The Revenge is out. Ranveer Singh returns as Hamza Ali Mazari in a darker, more brutal avatar as the sequel promises bigger action and higher stakes. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film releases on March 19, 2026.

Shikhar Pahariya shares rare pic to wish Janhvi Kapoor on birthday Janhvi Kapoor's birthday saw heartfelt wishes from Shikhar Pahariya and Khushi Kapoor, who shared rare family photos with Sridevi, while the actor also followed a meaningful personal tradition.

Aspirants Season 3 trailer unveils new battles beyond UPSC dreams The trailer of Aspirants Season 3 is out, teasing a new chapter where past ambitions meet present power as the characters face the realities of leadership and changing friendships.

Kohraa 2, Accused, Cheekatilo: Why morally complex women lead OTT's dark tales Indian OTT platforms are increasingly placing women at the centre of dark, psychologically intricate crime dramas. The streaming space is opening up for storytelling that risks focussing on female characters beyond stereotypes to lead layered and morally grey narratives.

Allu Sirish weds Nayanika Reddy: Ram Charan, Chief Minister Revanth Reddy present Allu Sirish and Nayanika Reddy were married in an intimate ceremony in Hyderabad attended by Ram Charan, Pawan Kalyan, Chiranjeevi, Suriya and Telangana CM Revanth Reddy.

Actor Arjun Das's Con City teaser promises quirky story with comedy and suspense The upcoming Tamil film Con City, directed by debutant Harish Durairaj, stars Arjun Das, Yogi Babu, Anna Ben and veteran actor Vadivukkarasi in the lead. The recently released title teaser shows the family face a life changing opportunity, promising fun and emotions amidst chaos.

Raja Shivaji, Cocktail 2 teasers to be attached to Dhurandhar 2 release: Report The teasers for the highly anticipated films Cocktail 2 and Raja Shivaji will be attached to the theatrical release of Dhurandhar 2. The move is expected to boost excitement for the two films.

Rajshri Deshpande calls for open conversations on cancer: Let's normalise this Actor Rajshri Deshpande opened up about her cancer diagnosis and emphasised the need to address the stigma around the disease. The actor, now recovering after surgery, also shared her plans to raise awareness.

MasterChef India 9: Nagpur brothers Ajinkya, Vikram Gandhe take home the trophy Nagpur's Vikram and Ajinkya Gandhe won MasterChef India Season 9 by impressing judges with their blend of traditional flavours and modern presentation. The brothers became the first finalists and brought the trophy home to Maharashtra's Orange City.

Mrithyunjay review: Promising ideas galore, but convenient writing lets film downRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Mrithyunjay movie review: Directed by Hussai Sha Kiran, Mrithyunjay is an investigative thriller that relies on the intellect of the hero and villain. While there are interesting ideas in the script, the film fails to explore its full potential.

Ash Santos on landing Marshals and learning patience in Hollywood Actor Ash Santos spoke about joining the Yellowstone universe with its spin-off, Marshals. In an exclusive interview, she recalled how rejection, a cancelled flight and a stressful screen test ultimately landed her the role of Andrea Cruz.

Project Hail Mary release pushed in India. Is it Dhurandhar 2 effect? Ryan Gosling's sci-fi film Project Hail Mary will now release in India six days after its US debut. The move is being rumoured to be an attempt to avoid a direct clash with Ranveer Singh's highly anticipated film Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Hello Bachhon review: TVF's show is less inspiration, more glorified ad campaignRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Actor Viineet Kumar Siingh plays Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey in TVF's latest series on India's exam culture. Also starring Vikram Kochhar and Girija Oak, the Netflix show struggles to move beyond hero worship to tell a nuanced story.

Vijay Deverakonda's uncle opens up on actor's humility after wedding Actor Vijay Deverakonda's uncle shared photos from the actor's post-wedding hometown visit, revealing a side of the star that fans rarely see.

YouTube removes Badshah's Tateeree after singer is booked for alleged obscenity YouTube has pulled down Badshah's new song Tateeree, with the rapper-singer facing legal action after a complaint was lodged that the track promotes obscenity and disrespects women.

BTS in India? Why 2026 seems the right time for K-pop superband's desi tour In 2026, BTS prepares to enter a new era with their latest album, ARIRANG, and the excitement, as everywhere, is at an all-time high among ARMY in India. On the other hand is India's booming live concert scenario. All things considered, this could be the right time for the Korean superband to hit the Indian stage.

Trisha shares photos after attending wedding with Vijay, thanks 'go-to people' Actor Trisha Krishnan shared solo pictures from a wedding reception in Chennai she attended with Vijay. The event comes amid public attention on Vijay's ongoing divorce proceedings after 27 years of marriage.

general news.headline: Iran President apologises to Gulf countries for attacks, says no intention to invadeIn a televised address, Pezeshkian said Iran's temporary leadership council had decided to suspend missile strikes against neighbouring states unless attacks on Iran originate from those countries.

Live: Several blasts rock Abu Dhabi, UAE activates air defence against missile threatUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Tensions in the Middle East escalated as the Israel Defense Forces reported multiple missile launches from Iran targeting the Greater Tel Aviv area, central Israel and southern regions including Be'er Sheva. Air raid sirens were activated across several areas, including Jerusalem, as defence systems moved to intercept the projectiles. Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia said its air defences intercepted four drones aimed at the Shaybah oil field in the Empty Quarter desert, hours after another drone attack was stopped near Riyadh. In Beirut, Israeli air strikes hit the southern suburbs after the military ordered a large-scale evacuation of the area, targeting sites linked to Hezbollah. The group later said its fighters clashed with Israeli troops near Nabi Sheet in Lebanon's Bekaa Valley. Elsewhere, authorities in Dubai reported a minor debris incident after an aerial interception, with no injuries. Iranian officials also rejected remarks by Donald Trump about surrender and leadership change, reaffirming that the country's leadership process is determined internally. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for live updates.

How India had the last laugh after Trump's Russian oil waiverHandcrafted ByOur Editors With global oil prices shooting through the roof due to the Iran war and the subsequent closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the US has fallen back on India to purchase sanctioned Russian crude to keep oil flowing into the global market. What's behind Trump's sudden change of heart?

Young cadets, SOS: Jaishankar explains why India let Iran ship dock in KochiThis is the first time that the External Affairs Minister has spoken on the issue that saw the Middle East conflict reach India's backyard. Jaishankar said India acted out of 'humanity' in allowing Iranian ship, IRIS Lavan, to dock at Kochi.

Live: Rapper-mayor Balen Shah's party wins 41 seats, leads in 78 seatsNepal Election Results 2026 Live Updates: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's newly launched Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) is steering toward a landslide victory in Nepal's first general elections since the Gen Z protests, breaking the stronghold of traditional parties in the politically fragile nation. According to the Election Commission's data available from 161 of the total 165 constituencies, the RSP has already won 41 seats and is leading in 78 more. The Nepali Congress has won 4 seats and is ahead in 11, while the CPN-UML has secured 2 seats and is leading in 8 constituencies. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

Indians have been good actors: Scott Bessent on Russian oil waiverUS Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Washington may lift sanctions on more Russian oil shipments, arguing that freeing stranded crude could quickly boost global supply and ease pressure on energy markets.

Anthropic data reveals 22 career options that remain safe from AIA new study by Anthropic suggests that while AI tools are spreading across workplaces, many professions still remain largely outside the reach of automation. The research, based on usage data from Claude, shows a wide gap between what AI can theoretically do and how much it is actually used in real jobs.

Kash Patel fires 10 FBI agents amid escalating Iran war. Here's whyLast month, FBI chief Kash Patel fired at least 10 agents linked to investigations into President Donald Trump, including the Mar-a-Lago classified documents probe. The dismissals came as the US-Israel strikes on Iran triggered retaliatory attacks by Tehran on US assets in the Middle East.

Man mowed down by train while chasing phone snatcher on tracks at Delhi stationA 22-year-old man was mowed down by a train on Friday evening while he was chasing a snatcher who had allegedly lifted his mobile phone at a railway station in Delhi.

From IAS to AI babu: Sarkari Naukri gets a tech upgradeHandcrafted ByOur Editors As thousands of aspirants celebrated the UPSC Civil Services results, the dream of a sarkari naukri once again dominated India's imagination. But the future of government service may look very different. The question we need to ask is this: in the age of artificial intelligence, is it possible that the next generation of babus may be coders?

When Earth gets hot by 1.5°C: The climate disasters waiting for IndiaThe world is already facing extreme consequences that are yet to reach the worst levels. But what exactly can India expect when the world fails to limit Earth's warming to 1.5°C?

sports.headline: Santner in Cummins mode before T20 World Cup final: Don't mind breaking hearts T20 World Cup final: Ahead of the much-anticipated T20 World Cup final against India, New Zealand skipper Mitchell Santner channelled his inner Pat Cummins and delivered a warning to the home fans in Ahmedabad.

Russell, Mercedes storm to AUS GP pole as Verstappen crashes out in qualifying Australian Grand Prix: George Russell and Mercedes laid down an early marker as the British driver secured pole position for the season-opening Australian GP on Saturday, March 7. It was an awful day for Max Verstappen, who crashed out early in qualifying.

Clutch is Hardik Pandya: How India all-rounder went into zen mode vs England T20 World Cup: Hardik Pandya revealed how he went into zen mode against England in the death overs to get the wicket of Sam Curran and run out Jacob Bethell in Mumbai. Hardik has been a key player in getting India to the final.

Wouldn't be surprised if Bumrah is T20 World Cup final's POTM: Clarke Former Australia captain Michael Clarke highlighted Jasprit Bumrah's match-winning abilities ahead of the T20 World Cup 2026 final, claiming that the star pacer could end up being the Player Of The Match in the India vs New Zealand clash in Ahmedabad.

Pakistan seal Hockey WC spot after 8 years, put AUS tour horror behind them Pakistan clinched qualification for the FIH Hockey World Cup 2026 with a late winner against Japan, while England dominated Malaysia to book their ticket. Pakistan make it to the World Cup after a gap of eight years.

Ahmedabad calling: Airlines add special flights for IND vs NZ final rush T20 World Cup: Flights and trains are being added as fans rush to Ahmedabad for the T20 World Cup final. With India chasing history and New Zealand eyeing their first title, travel demand for the big clash has surged.

Brendon McCullum set to continue as England coach despite Ashes humiliation Brendon McCullum is expected to remain as England's head coach across formats following a challenging winter, with strong support from players and the ECB amid ongoing reviews of recent performances.

T20 World Cup: A fresh mixed-soil surface at Ahmedabad could shape the T20 World Cup final between India and New Zealand. With pace, bounce and big hitters on both sides, the pitch may set up another high-scoring showdown.

With Varun losing grip, India must turn to Kuldeep Yadav in T20 World Cup finalT20 World Cup: With Varun Chakravarthy short of confidence and leaking runs, Jasprit Bumrah had to shoulder much of the responsibility. India may need to turn to Kuldeep Yadav in the final if they want to defend their title against New Zealand in Ahmedabad.

Iran players labelled wartime traitors after anthem silence in Asian Cup openerAn Iranian state TV presenter called the women's national football team "wartime traitors" after they did not sing the national anthem before an Asian Cup match against South Korea in Australia.

All England Open: Lakshya Sen storms into semis after thrashing World No. 6 LiIndia's Lakshya Sen continued his impressive run at the All England Open Badminton Championships as the 12th seed outclassed China's Li Shifeng in straight games to reach the men's singles semi-finals in Birmingham on Friday.

Amir slams Kaif: Be thankful to Sourav Ganguly, you played only because of fieldingT20 World Cup: Mohammad Amir slammed Mohammad Kaif, saying he has far more experience in T20 cricket than Kaif. Amir also added that Kaif should be grateful to Sourav Ganguly for giving him opportunities to play for India largely because of his fielding abilities.

Twelve ISL clubs accuse AIFF of profiting from 'crisis-hit season'Twelve ISL clubs wrote to the AIFF, alleging that the federation is set to earn a surplus of over Rs 3.4 crore from the ongoing season despite assuring the Sports Ministry it would not "make a rupee" from the league this year.

Glenn Phillips hopes New Zealand face a 'human' Jasprit Bumrah in finalJasprit Bumrah is a world-class operator, but even he can have an off day, something Glenn Phillips and New Zealand will be hoping for when they face India in the T20 World Cup final on Sunday.

I was nervous: Shivam Dube opens up on bowling nerve-wracking last over in semisT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Shivam Dube admitted he was nervous while bowling the final over against England, where he had to defend 30 runs, but said he backed his abilities as India won by seven runs to secure their place in the final.

WI remain stranded in Kolkata, four SA players likely to leave for New ZealandWest Indies players have been stranded in Kolkata for the fifth consecutive day after their exit from the T20 World Cup, as international airspace restrictions triggered by US and Israeli strikes on Iran continue to disrupt travel plans across the region.

Meme worthy: India Women trolled after horrible DRS call in Day-Night Perth TestINDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India Women wasted a review in the sixth over while attempting to dismiss Ellyse Perry on Day 1 of the pink-ball Test against Australia at the WACA in Perth on Friday.

Cristiano Ronaldo flies to Madrid for treatment as injury concerns deepenJorge Jesus, the head coach of Al-Nassr, has revealed that Cristiano Ronaldo's hamstring injury is more serious than initially expected, with the Portuguese star travelling to Madrid to undergo specialist treatment.

India Women chip away, but Australia stay ahead on pace-heavy day in Perth TestINDW vs AUSW: Australia had a steady opening day and trail by 102 runs with seven wickets in hand in the pink-ball Test. However, India will feel they remain in the contest, with the WACA pitch offering significant assistance to the fast bowlers.

Arvid Lindblad brings F1 excitement back to India at Red Bull Motojam 2026: In PhotosFrom drifting to stunt biking to an F1 car on Indian soil, Red Bull Moto Jam 2026 in Noida was a motorsport spectacle like no other ahead of the new F1 season.

India arrive in Ahmedabad as history beckons at T20 World Cup finalIND vs NZ, T20 World Cup 2026 Final: India arrived in Ahmedabad ahead of the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand at the Narendra Modi Stadium, chasing history as they bid to become the first team to win back-to-back titles.

Kapil Dev backs out-of-form Abhishek Sharma to play T20 World Cup finalKapil Dev said the Indian team management should retain Abhishek Sharma for the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand despite his struggles in the tournament so far.

Jasprit Bumrah is the 'best of all time': England's Brook after semi-final lossT20 World Cup: England captain Harry Brook hailed star Indian pacer Jasprit Bumrah as the best of all time after India's nail-biting seven-run win in the T20 World Cup semi-final clash at the Wankhede Stadium.

ICC names officials for India vs New Zealand T20 World Cup finalThe International Cricket Council has appointed Richard Illingworth and Alex Wharf as on-field umpires for the T20 World Cup 2026 final between Suryakumar Yadav's India and Mitchell Santner's New Zealand in Ahmedabad on Sunday.

Sanju saving his best for final: India star's childhood coach makes bold predictionT20 World Cup 2026: Sanju Samson's childhood coach George believes the wicketkeeper-batter has saved his best for the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand in Ahmedabad, backing him to continue his stellar form.

Jasprit Bumrah in a playing XI is like having a genie, a superpower: Faf du PlessisT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Faf du Plessis lauded Jasprit Bumrah for his impressive spell against England on Thursday at the Wankhede Stadium, which helped India qualify for the final against New Zealand on Sunday.

Leeds boss Farke handed one-match touchline ban after Man City defeatLeeds United manager Daniel Farke was handed a one-match ban and fined £8,000 for confronting match officials after the club's Premier League defeat to Manchester City.

T20 World Cup final fever: Western Railways add 2 special trains to AhmedabadWith the T20 World Cup final in mind, the Western Railways has decided to add two special superfast trains between Mumbai Central and Ahmedabad. India will take on New Zealand in the final on Sunday, March 8.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
